classDescription: A precision skirmisher built on stealth, expertise, and timing-sensitive burst damage.
roleIdentity: Skill specialist and precision striker: create openings, trigger Sneak Attack, and outmaneuver threats with cunning setup tools.

rogue-expertise-1.summary: Push chosen proficiencies into elite territory so your rogue feels exceptional, not just trained.
rogue-expertise-1.description: Expertise is one of the rogue's defining out-of-combat and scouting strengths. It makes the class feel dependable in the scenes that set fights up or solve problems before steel is drawn.

rogue-sneak-attack-1d6.summary: Once each turn, convert the right opening into extra precision damage instead of trading blows fairly.
rogue-sneak-attack-1d6.description:
Sneak Attack is your main combat loop: create or exploit an opening, land one quality hit, and convert it into precision burst damage.

Setup tools like Cunning Action, Steady Aim, ally positioning, and subclass tricks are there to make this trigger happen consistently.
rogue-sneak-attack-1d6.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-thieves-cant.summary: You understand and communicate through covert criminal shorthand, signs, and coded speech.
rogue-thieves-cant.description: Thieves' Cant reinforces the rogue fantasy outside combat. It matters in infiltration, underworld scenes, and any campaign where hidden information and coded messages matter.

rogue-cunning-action.summary: Turn movement and repositioning into a regular part of your round instead of a luxury.
rogue-cunning-action.description: Use Dash, Disengage, or Hide as a bonus action to control distance, escape danger, or create a Sneak Attack angle without spending your main action.

rogue-roguish-archetype-subclass.summary: Your archetype defines what kind of rogue you really are: killer, thief, arcane trickster, or another style of precision operator.
rogue-roguish-archetype-subclass.description: Roguish Archetype is the subclass gate for rogue. It should immediately shift how the sheet reads the character by introducing a stronger fantasy lane and the tools to support it.

rogue-steady-aim.summary: Slow down, line up the strike, and trade movement freedom for a cleaner chance to land your damage spike.
rogue-steady-aim.description: Use a bonus action to gain Advantage on your next attack this turn, but your speed becomes 0 until turn end. Use it when landing Sneak Attack is worth giving up movement.

rogue-cunning-strike.summary: Spend part of your Sneak Attack pressure on control or utility riders instead of raw damage alone.
rogue-cunning-strike.description: When Sneak Attack lands, you can trade part of its damage for tactical riders (such as control or positioning effects). Choose this when battlefield advantage is stronger than raw damage.

rogue-evasion.summary: Explosive effects that would ruin most characters become much less reliable against you.
rogue-evasion.description: Evasion is a signature rogue defensive passive. It rewards high Dexterity and reinforces the idea that the class survives by skillful movement rather than raw durability.

rogue-reliable-talent.summary: Your practiced skills become difficult to sabotage with bad luck alone.
rogue-reliable-talent.description: Reliable Talent is one of the rogue's biggest quality-of-life spikes for noncombat play. It turns trained expertise into true consistency and should be reflected clearly in how the sheet explains skill outcomes.

rogue-improved-cunning-strike.summary: Your precision damage riders become more flexible and punishing as your control options deepen.
rogue-improved-cunning-strike.description: Your Cunning Strike options improve, letting you convert Sneak Attack damage into stronger control outcomes when needed. This deepens your turn-by-turn tactical choices.

rogue-devious-strikes.summary: Your dirty-fighting toolkit becomes nastier and more layered, pushing the rogue deeper into battlefield manipulation.
rogue-devious-strikes.description: Your precision riders become nastier. At this tier, you should regularly decide between maximum damage and disruptive effects that set up the party or protect your next turn.

rogue-slippery-mind.summary: Your mental defenses harden so hostile influence has a harder time pinning you down.
rogue-slippery-mind.description: Slippery Mind is a late-game defensive upgrade that helps rogues stay functional against spell-heavy encounters and mental pressure.

rogue-subtle-strikes.summary: You find cleaner openings against distracted enemies and turn pressure into easier precision damage.
rogue-subtle-strikes.description: Subtle Strikes improves how often the rogue can cash in on the class's signature damage engine without needing perfect circumstances every round.

rogue-elusive.summary: You become frustratingly hard to pin down, making direct offense against you less reliable.
rogue-elusive.description: Elusive is a high-level defensive identity feature. By this point the rogue should feel like a nightmare target to read and punish cleanly.

rogue-stroke-of-luck.summary: When the moment is too important to leave to chance, you can simply force success where it matters most.
rogue-stroke-of-luck.description: Stroke of Luck is the rogue capstone fantasy: supreme clutch reliability when a key attack misses or a vital check nearly fails.

rogue-expertise-2.summary: Double down on two more signature proficiencies so your skill ceiling stays exceptional deep into the campaign.
rogue-expertise-2.description: The rogue gets a second wave of Expertise because the class is meant to stay the table's specialist, not flatten into generic competence at higher levels.

rogue-sneak-attack-l3.summary: Once each turn, convert the right opening into extra precision damage instead of trading blows fairly.
rogue-sneak-attack-l3.description: This level increases your Sneak Attack dice. Keep prioritizing setup and positioning so your once-per-turn precision burst lands as often as possible.
rogue-sneak-attack-l3.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l5.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l7.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l9.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l11.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l13.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l19.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-sneak-attack-l20.summary: Once each turn, convert the right opening into extra precision damage instead of trading blows fairly.
rogue-sneak-attack-l20.description: This level increases your Sneak Attack dice. Keep prioritizing setup and positioning so your once-per-turn precision burst lands as often as possible.
rogue-sneak-attack-l20.effect: Once per turn when you hit with a Finesse or ranged weapon and have Advantage (or an adjacent ally with no Disadvantage), add your current Sneak Attack dice.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l4.summary: Increase your key ability scores or take a feat to sharpen your rogue plan.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l4.description: This level is a build-shaping checkpoint. Most rogues raise Dexterity first, but this can also be where you lock in a feat that strengthens setup, stealth, or survivability.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l8.summary: Another ability or feat breakpoint to tighten your precision and survivability profile.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l8.description: Use this checkpoint to reinforce your role: higher Dexterity for attack quality and AC, stronger Constitution for durability, or a feat that supports your preferred rogue loop.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l12.summary: Mid-game build spike to keep your rogue scaling with encounter pressure.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l12.description: At this tier, ASI or feat choices should support the way you actually play: accuracy, stealth reliability, mobility, or tactical control options from your subclass.

rogue-ability-score-improvement-l16.summary: Late-game build adjustment so your rogue remains sharp in high-tier play.
rogue-ability-score-improvement-l16.description: Use this level to patch a weakness or double down on your strongest lane. High-level rogues feel best when their setup, damage delivery, and defenses all stay reliable.

rogue-subclass-feature-l10.summary: Your archetype adds a new defining tool that should change how your turns or missions are planned.
rogue-subclass-feature-l10.description: This is not filler progression. Level 10 is where your chosen rogue archetype deepens into a clearer gameplay identity in combat, infiltration, or utility play.

rogue-subclass-feature-l14.summary: Your subclass package gains another major identity upgrade.
rogue-subclass-feature-l14.description: By now your archetype should strongly shape your turn planning and role in scenes. This feature is part of what separates Assassin, Thief, and Arcane Trickster in real play.

rogue-subclass-feature-l15.summary: High-tier subclass payoff that pushes your archetype into endgame territory.
rogue-subclass-feature-l15.description: This level usually unlocks one of your subclass’s most impactful tactical tools. Expect it to matter in boss encounters and high-stakes mission moments.

rogue-epic-boon-l19.summary: You gain an Epic Boon on top of your Sneak Attack increase.
rogue-epic-boon-l19.description: This endgame milestone combines top-tier precision damage with a powerful boon pick, letting you tailor one final major edge before capstone play.

progressionSummary.summary: Roguish Archetype (Subclass), Steady Aim, Sneak Attack (2d6).

buildTips.primaryReason: powers attacks, Armor Class, stealth, and initiative
buildTips.secondaryReason: extra HP helps you survive when plans go wrong
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: Perception and common saves use Wisdom more than many new rogues expect
buildTips.playPatternHint: Use Cunning Action or Steady Aim to set up one high-value Sneak Attack each turn; spend Cunning Strike riders when control is worth more than raw damage.
